2026-04-07T05:46:07.8008369Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m

2026-04-07T05:46:07.8009003Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m

The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). 2026-04-07T05:50:20.2232434Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-07T05:46:46.07415339Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57850,\"title\":\"[CosmosDBForPostgreSql] Docs: Add service-specific examples, key concepts, and code snippets to README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/cosmosdbforpostgresql/Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit e373db219 (#57114) by `@haiyuan_zhang`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nThe MPG migration for CosmosDB for PostgreSQL was completed, adding `ci.mgmt.yml` and `test-resources.json` to establish the TypeSpec-based CI pipeline. The package has 51 generated files covering clusters, servers, configurations, firewall rules, coordinator/node configurations, private endpoint connections, and roles — but the README remains a generic boilerplate with no service-specific content.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **No service-specific key concepts**: The \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section links only to a generic Azure SDK page; it does not describe CosmosDB for PostgreSQL resources (Cluster, Server, FirewallRule, Configuration, CoordinatorConfiguration, NodeConfiguration, PrivateEndpointConnection, Role)\\n- **No code examples in README**: The \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section links only to a generic Azure SDK samples page; no inline code snippets showing how to create a cluster, list servers, manage firewall rules, or update configurations\\n- **No test-backed snippets**: There is no `tests/Samples/` directory and no `#region Snippet:` tags anywhere in the package — the snippet tooling (`Update-Snippets.ps1`) cannot inject any examples\\n- **CHANGELOG 1.1.0-beta.3 is empty**: The upcoming unreleased version section has blank `Features Added`, `Breaking Changes`, `Bugs Fixed`, and `Other Changes` entries even though the TypeSpec migration may have introduced API surface changes\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File: `sdk/cosmosdbforpostgresql/Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql/README.md`\\n\\n**Edit** the README to replace the generic sections with service-specific content.\\n\\nReplace the generic \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThe `Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql` library provides resource management for [Azure Cosmos DB for PostgreSQL]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted), a managed service for running PostgreSQL workloads at scale.\\n\\n### Resource hierarchy\\n\\n- **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlCluster** — The top-level resource representing a cluster (a group of nodes). Create, update, restart, or delete a cluster and promote replicas to independent read-write clusters.\\n- **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterServer** — Represents an individual server (coordinator or worker) within a cluster.\\n- **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlFirewallRule** — Manages firewall rules that control external access to the cluster.\\n- **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlConfiguration** — A cluster-level configuration parameter applying to all nodes.\\n- **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlCoordinatorConfiguration** — A configuration parameter scoped to the coordinator node.\\n- **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlNodeConfiguration** — A configuration parameter scoped to worker nodes.\\n- **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlPrivateEndpointConnection** — Manages private endpoint connections for the cluster.\\n- **CosmosDBForPostgreSqlRole** — Manages database roles (users) within the cluster.\\n```\\n\\nReplace the generic \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following sections show code samples for common management scenarios.\\n\\n### Create a cluster\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### List all clusters in a subscription\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Create a firewall rule\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Update a configuration parameter\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate the file `sdk/cosmosdbforpostgresql/Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql/tests/Samples/Sample1_Clusters.cs` with NUnit test methods that wrap sample code in `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion` tags.\\n\\n**File to create:** `sdk/cosmosdbforpostgresql/Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql/tests/Samples/Sample1_Clusters.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql.Models;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_Clusters : SamplesBase\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateCluster()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateCosmosDBForPostgreSqlCluster\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n            // Retrieve the subscription and resource group\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;;\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await client\\n                .GetSubscriptionResource(new ResourceIdentifier($\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\\u0026#34;))\\n                .GetResourceGroupAsync(resourceGroupName);\\n\\n            // Prepare cluster create parameters\\n            CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterData data = new CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterData(\\n                new Azure.Core.AzureLocation(\\u0026#34;eastus\\u0026#34;))\\n            {\\n                PostgresqlVersion = \\u0026#34;15\\u0026#34;,\\n                AdministratorLoginPassword = \\u0026#34;myP@ssword1\\u0026#34;,\\n                CoordinatorStorageQuotaInMb = 524288,\\n                CoordinatorVCores = 4,\\n                NodeCount = 2,\\n                NodeStorageQuotaInMb = 524288,\\n                NodeVCores = 4,\\n            };\\n\\n            CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterCollection clusters = resourceGroup.GetCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusters();\\n            ArmOperation operation = await clusters\\n                .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myCluster\\u0026#34;, data);\\n            CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterResource cluster = operation.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Cluster provisioning state: {cluster.Data.ProvisioningState}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListClusters()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ListCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusters\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\\u0026#34;;\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client\\n                .GetSubscriptionResource(new ResourceIdentifier($\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\\u0026#34;))\\n                .GetAsync();\\n\\n            await foreach (CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterResource cluster in subscription\\n                .GetCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClustersAsync())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine(cluster.Data.Name);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateFirewallRule()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateCosmosDBForPostgreSqlFirewallRule\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;;\\n            string clusterName = \\u0026#34;myCluster\\u0026#34;;\\n\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await client\\n                .GetSubscriptionResource(new ResourceIdentifier($\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\\u0026#34;))\\n                .GetResourceGroupAsync(resourceGroupName);\\n\\n            CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterResource cluster = await resourceGroup\\n                .GetCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterAsync(clusterName);\\n\\n            CosmosDBForPostgreSqlFirewallRuleData ruleData =\\n                new CosmosDBForPostgreSqlFirewallRuleData(\\u0026#34;0.0.0.0\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;255.255.255.255\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            ArmOperation operation = await cluster\\n                .GetCosmosDBForPostgreSqlFirewallRules()\\n                .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;allowAll\\u0026#34;, ruleData);\\n\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Firewall rule provisioning state: {operation.Value.Data.ProvisioningState}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task UpdateConfiguration()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:UpdateCosmosDBForPostgreSqlConfiguration\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;;\\n            string clusterName = \\u0026#34;myCluster\\u0026#34;;\\n            string configurationName = \\u0026#34;array_nulls\\u0026#34;;\\n\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await client\\n                .GetSubscriptionResource(new ResourceIdentifier($\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\\u0026#34;))\\n                .GetResourceGroupAsync(resourceGroupName);\\n\\n            CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterResource cluster = await resourceGroup\\n                .GetCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterAsync(clusterName);\\n\\n            CosmosDBForPostgreSqlConfigurationResource configuration = await cluster\\n                .GetCosmosDBForPostgreSqlConfigurationAsync(configurationName);\\n\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Configuration \\u0026#39;{configurationName}\\u0026#39; current value: {configuration.Data.Value}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Snippet names and their README placeholder locations:**\\n\\n| Snippet name | README section | .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myCluster\\u0026#34;, data);\\n            CosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusterResource cluster = operation.Value;\\n verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListClusters()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ListCosmosDBForPostgreSqlClusters\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\\u0026#34;;\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client\\n                .GetSubscriptionResource(new ResourceIdentifier($\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\\u0026#34;))\\n                .GetAsync();\\n\\n            await foreach
